game_info.title: "The Vanishing Journalist",
game_info.concept: **无处遁形：全网公敌 (Unseen: Hacker's Prey)** Players assume the role of a hacker, interacting with a simulated desktop interface primarily through taps to open applications like a web browser, database, and chat client. The core loop involves using the browser to search for keywords, extracting information (e.g., names, dates) to input into the database for filtering, and then using these clues to generate passwords for cracking target accounts (e.g., email, social media) with a limited number of attempts. Successful account access unlocks new information, which can be used in the chat interface to social engineer targets by selecting optimal dialogue options from a list of 3-5 choices. Progression is chapter-based, with each of the 5 main cases and 2 DLCs unlocking sequentially upon successful completion, and new advanced hacking tools become available as the player advances through the narrative. The game concludes when the player successfully uncovers the truth of a case, or fails to gather critical evidence, leading to an inconclusive or negative narrative outcome.
game_info.description: You are a skilled hacker investigating a conspiracy. Use your desktop interface to search the web for clues, query databases to filter information, and crack passwords to access hidden accounts. Once inside, use the chat application to social engineer targets by selecting the right dialogue options. Each successful hack reveals more of the mystery. Complete all objectives to uncover the truth, but beware - too many failed password attempts will trigger security alerts and you'll lose the case!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Navigate between desktop applications and menu options
Space: Select/Open application or confirm choice
Z: Go back/Cancel action
Enter: Start game from menu
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart and return to main menu

TEST_1.test_description: Basic interaction test. Tests navigation between applications, opening and closing windows, and basic UI interaction without attempting to solve puzzles.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Navigate through all desktop applications in sequence (Browser, Database, Chat, Email). Open each one, wait briefly, then close and move to the next. Repeat this cycle to validate UI responsiveness.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: All applications should open and close without errors. Navigation should be smooth and responsive. No crashes or visual glitches. Test runs for 300 frames minimum.

TEST_2.test_description: Win condition test. Tests the complete game flow from start to finish by solving the first case optimally using pre-programmed knowledge of the correct answers.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Successfully complete Case 1 by cracking the target's email and obtaining confession through chat. Game should transition to GAME_OVER_WIN state. Victory message should display with case summary.
